title: Supply Planner
description: Master the full planning process — from S&OP and S&OE through daily execution — then understand how Planning Software and ERP support each step of the way.

phases.title: Process Foundation
phases.description: Understand how S&OP, S&OE, and Execution connect into a single planning operating model — and what your role looks like inside each cycle.

sections.title: The Planning Operating Model
sections.description: How S&OP, S&OE, and Execution relate to each other — the three planning horizons and the cadence that connects them.

sections.title: S&OP — Monthly Planning
sections.description: The full monthly S&OP cycle — what it produces, who participates, and how to run each step from demand review through executive sign-off.

sections.title: S&OE — Weekly Execution
sections.description: The weekly S&OE cycle — monitoring the near-term plan, resolving exceptions, and handing off cleanly to execution.

sections.title: Execution — Daily Operations
sections.description: How confirmed plans become shipped orders — order prioritisation, real-time visibility, execution discipline, and feeding actuals back to planning.

phases.title: Understanding the Tools
phases.description: Learn how Planning Software and ERP support the processes from Phase 1 — from how the planning engine models your supply chain to how you work in both systems day-to-day.

sections.title: Planning Software — Building Blocks
sections.description: The core objects the planning engine uses to model your supply chain: items, locations, BOMs, BODs, resources, and how they form a network.

sections.title: Planning Software — Data Flow & Logic
sections.description: How demand propagates upstream and supply flows downstream through the network, and the key planning logic rules that shape the output.

sections.title: Planning Software — Simulations & Navigation
sections.description: How to run workflow simulations in Planning Software and navigate the interface to read and act on plan output.

sections.title: ERP — Understanding the System
sections.description: What ERP is, how it structures data, and what it sends back to Planning Software — the signals that keep the plan grounded in reality.

sections.title: ERP — Working in ERP
sections.description: The business logic, key workflows, and navigation a supply planner uses in ERP — releasing orders, managing inventory, and reading output.

phases.title: Advanced Planning
phases.description: Go beyond the baseline plan — use scenario planning to stress-test supply risks and demand uncertainty, set the policies that govern system behaviour, and measure planning performance.

sections.title: Scenario Planning Fundamentals
sections.description: What scenario planning is, when to use it, and how to set up scenarios that surface the decisions worth making.

sections.title: Running Scenarios in Planning Software
sections.description: The end-to-end practical workflow — creating, adjusting, running, comparing, and promoting scenarios within Planning Software.

sections.title: Planning Policy
sections.description: The documented decisions that govern system behaviour — service level targets, safety stock rules, allocation logic, and prioritisation frameworks.

sections.title: Governance & KPIs
sections.description: Decision frameworks, escalation paths, exception-based working, and the KPI structure that connects performance measurement to the S&OP cycle.
